issue_body:
```
Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "<string>", line 20, in <module>
File "/tmp/pip-build-75nsmtd3/fake-factory/setup.py", line 9, in <module>
NEWS = open(os.path.join(here, 'CHANGELOG.rst')).read()
File "/home/travis/build/DRMacIver/hypothesis/.tox/py34/lib/python3.4/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc3 in position 100: ordinal not in range(128)
```

Basically, you need to open this with an encoding parameter, as it's non-ascii. In order to make this work across Python 2 and 3 you probably want [io.open](https://docs.python.org/2/library/io.html#io.open).

issue_title: ValueError: could not broadcast input array from shape (11,11) into shape (11)
issue_body:
[testarr.txt](https://github.com/tBuLi/symfit/files/1928380/testarr.txt)
I have the following code to fit a 2D gaussian:

```python
from symfit import Variable, Parameter, Fit, exp
import numpy as np


arr = np.genfromtxt('testarr.txt')


a = Parameter(name='a', value=8000)
b = Parameter(name='b', value=3000, min=100)
sig_x = Parameter(name='sig_x', value=2)
sig_y = Parameter(name='sig_y', value=1.5)
mu_x = Parameter(name='mu_x', value=0.5)
mu_y = Parameter(name='mu_y', value=0.5)
x_var = Variable(name='x_var')
y_var = Variable(name='y_var')
z_var = Variable(name='z_var')

model = {z_var: ((a*exp(-(((x_var-mu_x)**2/(2*sig_x**2)) + ((y_var-mu_y)**2 / (2*sig_y**2))))))+b}
shape = arr.shape

xv, yv = np.meshgrid(*[np.arange(1 - np.floor(x/2), x - np.floor(x/2) + 1) for x in shape])
fit = Fit(model, z_var=arr, y_var=yv, x_var=xv)
res = fit.execute()

print(res)
```

Which gives this error: 

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Program Files\JetBrains\PyCharm 2018.1\helpers\pydev\pydev_run_in_console.py", line 53, in run_file
    pydev_imports.execfile(file, globals, locals)  # execute the script
  File "C:\Program Files\JetBrains\PyCharm 2018.1\helpers\pydev\_pydev_imps\_pydev_execfile.py", line 18, in execfile

  File "C:/Users/Smit/_processed_data/2018/20180419_psf/fitting.py", line 29, in <module>
    res = fit.execute()
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\py_main\lib\site-packages\symfit\core\fit.py", line 1364, in execute
    cov_matrix = self.covariance_matrix(dict(zip(self.model.params, minimizer_ans._popt)))
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\py_main\lib\site-packages\symfit\core\fit.py", line 850, in covariance_matrix
    return self._cov_mat_equal_lenghts(best_fit_params=best_fit_params)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\py_main\lib\site-packages\symfit\core\fit.py", line 914, in _cov_mat_equal_lenghts
    jac = np.array(self.model.eval_jacobian(**kwargs))

issue_title: Option --timeout
aredis pipeline does not work with Python 3.5.1
issue_body:
## Checklist

- Python version 3.5.1
- Does issue exists against the `master` branch of aredis? Yes

## Steps to reproduce
Use aredis and execute a pipeline with Python 3.5.1.

## Expected behavior
No error.

## Actual behavior
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "c:\Users\2\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python35-32\Lib\asyncio\tasks.py", line 239, in _step
    result = coro.send(None)
  File "C:\prj\paintman\paintman\socket\async_socket_server.py", line 63, in process_queue
    commands = await async_read_server_command_chunk()
  File "C:\prj\paintman\paintman\socket\redis_protocol.py", line 89, in async_read_server_command_chunk
    res = await p.execute()
  File "C:\prj\paintman\env\lib\site-packages\aredis\pipeline.py", line 286, in execute
    return await exec(conn, stack, raise_on_error)
  File "C:\prj\paintman\env\lib\site-packages\aredis\pipeline.py", line 203, in _execute_transaction
    if isinstance(response, typing.Awaitable):
AttributeError: module 'typing' has no attribute 'Awaitable'
```

aredis will check being awaitable in this line of code:

https://github.com/NoneGG/aredis/blob/f662f3332021a1147ed55cd5a202aa9b37c44d0b/aredis/pipeline.py#L203

and from what I understand, `typing.Awaitable` is not available in all Python 3.5 versions (source: https://docs.python.org/3.5/whatsnew/changelog.html#id22). You could use `inspect` package to check an object being awaitable or not:
```
import inspect
if inspect.isawaitable(response):
    ...
```

I decided to test out what colors worked and what colors didn't when I changed them with `ds4drv` and these are what worked for me since it's all HEX Codes, not every color shows up well as the LED seems to be limited to a set number of colors.

```
LED Color Profiles:

 Lightbar OFF  = 000000

 Blue (def.)   = 0000ff / 000fff / 110196 / 070496
 Red           = ff0000 / ff0f0f / 340000 / 990000
 Yellow        = fff000 / ffff00 / f0ff0f / f0ff00 / 678901
 White         = fffff0 / ffffff / fff0ff / f0ffff / f0f0f0
 Pink          = ff00ff / ff0fff / ff00c1 / fd00ff / 330033
 Grey ?        = 0f0f0f
 Green         = 00ff00 / 199600 / 006300 / 00cc00 / 66cc00
 Teal          = 1fca1f / 008080
 Light Blue    = 123456 / 112233 / 4682b4 / 66cccc
 Peach         = 8b4513 / 993333 / cc6633 / ff3333 / cc821a
 Purple        = 660033 / 990066 / 990099

```

there isn't much color variation differences in these HEX Codes, but they all seem to produce the colors I've listed. issue_title: Crash (heap corruption) in keyring call under TortoiseHg 2.3
issue_body:
I am trying to setup keyring extension included in TortoiseHG. So I added the following lines in .hg\hgrc:

    
    [auth]
    flv.schemes = https
    flv.prefix = hg.fastlaneventures.ru
    flv.username = vosipov
    

The command hg in --debug gives the following result:

    
    using https://hg.fastlaneventures.ru/flv-docs/flv-docs/
    sending capabilities command
    using auth.flv.* for authentication
    hg.fastlaneventures.ru certificate matched fingerprint 02:c4:f7:e3:52:8f:94:f8:9b:ad:a4:e3:20:58:4a:b5:80:8f:09:36
    [HgKeyring] Keyring URL: https://hg.fastlaneventures.ru
    [HgKeyring] Username found in .hg/hgrc: vosipov
    [HgKeyring] Looking for password for user vosipov and url https://hg.fastlaneventures.ru
    

And after that the window appears "The program "Mercurial Distributed SCM" doesn't work". And password is not remembered in keyring - every time I executed pull/push I need to enter login & password.

After some investigation the problem I found the following. In the keyring module in the backend.py module in the WinVaultKeyring class in the _get_password function there is a line "res = self.win32cred.CredRead(Type=self.win32cred.CRED_TYPE_GENERIC, TargetName=target,)". The program crash exactly in this line. But if this line execute in console with the same parameters (for example, res = self.win32cred.CredRead("BAUWENS\vosipov", self.win32cred.CRED_TYPE_GENERIC)) then no crash.

In the Windows Events Log catched exception when application crashed - 0xc0000374 STATUS_HEAP_CORRUPTION.

Now I am trying to compile the pywin32 to continue investigation.

I registered this issue in TortoiseHG Bugs ([[/tortoisehg/thg/issue/1690|here]]), but they said it is python keyring issue. 

Environment:
* keyring-0.8
* OS: Windows 7 Pro SP1
* TortoiseHG 2.3

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/58
- Originally reported by: [aristanalytic](http://bitbucket.org/aristanalytic)
- Originally created at: 2012-03-05T06:06:00.560

issue_title: Maybe add a way to check if one item of a list of values exists
issue_body:
Raised in https://forum.m-siemens.de/d/29-how-to-use-matches-with-int-values

Basically, with `any` and `all` we check, if a document's field – which is a list of values – contains a given value or only is a given value. From the docs:

```python
>>> db.search(User.groups.any(['admin', 'sudo']))
[{'name': 'user2', 'groups': ['admin', 'user']},
 {'name': 'user3', 'groups': ['sudo', 'user']}]

>>> db.search(User.groups.all(['admin', 'user']))
[{'name': 'user2', 'groups': ['admin', 'user']}]
```

Note how in this case the field we check is a list. Now, right now we don't have a way for the reverse operation: if the field is a value, check if it's contained in a list. It could be something like this:

```python
>>> db.search(User.name.in(['admin', 'user']))
[{'name': 'admin'}]
[{'name': 'user'}]
```

The issue is mainly naming. We can't name the method `in` as it's a Python keyword. I'm torn between calling it `part_of` or `contained_in` but both aren't exactly really intuitive when I think about it.

---

By the way, the workaround for now is using a custom test function:

```python
>>> db.search(User.name.test(lambda name: name in ['admin', 'user']))
[{'name': 'admin'}]
[{'name': 'user'}]
```

issue_title: Query validation error on nested record
issue_body:
# Short description

When the graph contains nested fields of type `Record`, validation considers the query which requests specific fields of nested record, invalid.

# Reproducing

## Environment

```
Python 3.6.4
```

## Dependencies
(obtained by `pip install hiku graphql-core`)

```
graphql-core==2.0
Hiku==0.4.1
promise==2.1
Rx==1.6.0
six==1.11.0
typing==3.6.2
```

## Code

```python
import sys

from hiku.graph import Graph, Root, Field
from hiku.types import Record, String, Integer

from hiku.validate.query import QueryValidator
from hiku.readers.graphql import read


def config_resolver(fields):
    return [{
        'app': {
            'host': 'trunk.example.com',
            'port': 80,
        },
    }]


AppConfig = Record[{'host': String, 'port': Integer}]
Config = Record[{'app': AppConfig}]


graph = Graph([
    Root([
        Field('config', Config, config_resolver),
    ])
])


query = '''
  query {
    config {
      app { host port }
    }
  }
'''
query = read(query)


validator = QueryValidator(graph)
validator.visit(query)
if validator.errors.list:
    print('ERROR!', validator.errors.list)
    sys.exit(1)
print('OK')
```

## Actual result

Script exists with non-zero exit code and produces the following output with cryptic error message:

```
ERROR! ['Not a link']
```

## Expected result

Query considered valid and script produces the following output:

```
OK
```

issue_title: _GetDescInvokeType regression on dynamic.py
issue_body:
Regarding this [commit](https://github.com/mhammond/pywin32/commit/a22ddedfd0d1a117c95876bf7e25e2dd4a419604#diff-f7b7ba0d109e33715b45f2165b6ba329) We found a regression when trying to set ```AdminQueueInfo``` on an ```MSMQ.MSMQMessage``` as shown in this sample : 
from win32com.client import Dispatch
import pythoncom

def _GetDescInvokeTypeNew(entry, invoke_type):
    # determine the wFlags argument passed as input to IDispatch::Invoke
    if not entry or not entry.desc: return invoke_type
    varkind = entry.desc[4] # from VARDESC struct returned by ITypeComp::Bind
    if varkind == pythoncom.VAR_DISPATCH and invoke_type == pythoncom.INVOKE_PROPERTYGET:
        return pythoncom.INVOKE_FUNC | invoke_type # DISPATCH_METHOD & DISPATCH_PROPERTYGET can be combined in IDispatch::Invoke
    else:
        return invoke_type

def _GetDescInvokeTypeOld(entry, default_invoke_type):
    if not entry or not entry.desc: return default_invoke_type
    return entry.desc[4]

queue = Dispatch("MSMQ.MSMQQueueInfo")
message = Dispatch("MSMQ.MSMQMessage")

attr = 'AdminQueueInfo'
message.__LazyMap__(attr)
entry = message._olerepr_.propMapPut[attr]

invoke_type = _GetDescInvokeTypeNew(entry, pythoncom.INVOKE_PROPERTYPUT)
message._oleobj_.Invoke(entry.dispid, 0, invoke_type, 0, "TOTO")
Previous code was returning ```8```, corresponding to ```entry.desc[4]```. New code is returning ```invoke_type = INVOKE_PROPERTYPUT```, so ```4```, that made invoke crash. I'm not expert so I'm not understanding the underlying reason of this update, but what do you think of this change : https://github.com/GitBadSanta/pywin32/commit/6b4dcb9b5609d3b6dd210b148f1d11f48f751200 PS : This is covering issue [1194](https://github.com/mhammond/pywin32/issues/1194)

issue_title: Virtualenv/venv and uninstall support for the postinstall script
issue_body:
The readme mentions:

> However, you almost certainly need to execute:
>    `python Scripts/pywin32_postinstall.py -install`
> after installation, or you will see various ImportErrors importing core modules.

It's not clear how this interacts with use of pywin32 from within a virtualenv, or with uninstallation. Can this be clarified (and if needed, support be added)?

Specifically:

1. If I run the postinstall, then uninstall pywin32, will there be any "left over" settings? How can those be cleared up?
2. If I create a virtual environment, install pywin32 and run the postinstall, then delete the virtualenv (without uninstalling anything) will this leave my system clean?
3. If I create *two* virtual environments, and install pywin32 in each, do I need to run the postinstall from each?

Note that for (2) and (3), I'd be perfectly happy for 99% of use cases to simply have a clearly documented list of "things that don't work without postinstall", combined with a note that the postinstall should not be run in a virtual environment. While it would be nice to (for example) host a service in a virtualenv, it's not something I explicitly need, whereas firing up a temporary virtualenv, installing some packages in it, using them, and then deleting it, is something I do routinely.

issue_title: Snowflake IDs should not be strings
issue_body:
Currently IDs are implemented as `str` because that is what the API returns it as. This can lead to some confusion or storage woes depending.

In reality, IDs are snowflakes which are 64-bit unsigned integers. So they should ideally be at least `int`. This comes with certain benefits:
- Faster hashing (O(1) vs O(n))
- Faster comparisons (O(1) vs O(n))
- Less memory consumption (28-36 bytes vs 49 + 16-19 bytes)

The standard `json` module allows for 64-bit integers as values, but the `json` module does not allow `int` keys since the JSON spec only allows string keys. Due to this, there may be some woes from the user end if they're using JSON storage using IDs as keys. Said users should cast it to `str` instead.

A proposed question must be taken into consideration before going through with this change in the future:
- Should the ID type be `int` or a subclass of `int` (i.e. `Snowflake`) with certain member functions?

The memory consumption of an ID if the type was `int` would be 28-36 bytes but it would increase to 52-60 bytes if it was a subclass. Otherwise the subclass would behave the same as the `int`.

issue_title: Possible to save frames from videos with a high fps?
issue_body:
```
from time import time
import mss
import mss.tools

start = time()
count = 1

while True:
	with mss.mss() as sct:
	    # The screen part to capture
	    monitor = {'top': 144, 'left': 80, 'width': 1397, 'height': 782}
	    output = 'frames/sct-{}.png'.format(count)
	    count += 1
	    # Grab the data
	    sct_img = sct.grab(monitor)

	    # Save to the picture file
	    mss.tools.to_png(sct_img.rgb, sct_img.size, output)
	    # print(output)
	    now = time()
	    if (now - start) % 60 >= 10: break
```
This seems to save only around 100 frames for 10 seconds. Can this be improved?

```code

issue_title: Wake up all coroutines in protocol.Stream after connection was lost
issue_body:
On the server-side this is not very critical, because all request handlers are cancelled in case of connection lost. But on the client-side it is possible to hang forever.

List of awaitable things client and server can still await after connection was lost:

- `protocol.Stream.__headers__.get()`
- `protocol.Stream.__buffer__.read()`
- `protocol.Stream._connection.write_ready.wait()`
- `protocol.Stream.__window_updated__.wait()`
- `protocol.Stream._connection.outbound_streams_limit.wait()`

All coroutines, which are awaiting these things should be awaken and they should raise exception.

Possible solution: see [async-timeout](https://github.com/aio-libs/async-timeout) for implementation details. It is possible to keep track of all currently running tasks (e.g. `recv_headers`) and cancel them. But `Task.cancel()` will loose all details and it is hard to debug such errors.

issue_title: automated ensembling v4
issue_body:
When training up an algorithm, it can be more accurate to train algorithms on subsets of the problem, rather than the whole problem. 

So, for instance, if we want to predict temperatures, we can either feed in `cloud_cover` as a categorical variable, and train up one monolithic model for all different types of cloud cover. Or, we can split up our data into 3 different datasets for the three different `cloud_cover` categories, and train a model on each dataset individually.

The split and train 3 models approach will oftentimes end up with a more accurate forecast, but of course, at the expense of considerably more complexity. 

Explore doing this in an automate way in auto_ml. 

create something like `train_categorical_ensemble`. you will have to feed in `categorical_column`. Then, we will split the dataset on that, train a different algorithm on each category, and then store all of those trained models. Then, we'll have to build in some extra logic. We'll have to create something like `load_keras_model` for `load_category_split_models`. Part of that function will include loading up all of our trained models for each category. But another part of that function will have to include ensembling them together. 

Essentially, we'll have a special `.predict` function. You can still pass in either a single dictionary or a dataframe. For a single dictionary, we'll find it's category based on `categorical_column`, choose the right model for that category, and return that prediction. For a dataframe, we'll just convert it to a list of dictionaries first, then follow the same logic. We can make this faster at the cost of more complexity, but I think the convert to list of dictionaries approach should work just fine for an initial implementation.

issue_body:
Hey,

this is quite some awesome code you put together. I would like to propose an improvement:

Assuming the following scenario: 
I have data from three years and I would like to divide them into three parts, so in the end I have a word cloud with the upper 33% being from data from the first year, the middle 33% from the second year and the bottom 33% being words from the third year and in between the data there is a smooth transition of words.

Currently I'm doing this by creating a word cloud applying the years to three different masks (the top, middle and bottom 33%) and put it together into a full cloud, leaving visible seperation marks in the result (between the slices).

So I thought, I can code this by my own, but I cant. However I thought of three possible ways to do this:

1. Input a `list of tuples` or a `dataframe` with the left part containing a string and the right part a datetime.
2. Input as a list of `n` parts, e.g. `n=3` like above, so it looks like this `[[a, b, a, f], [g, e, a], [b, b, g, a]]`.
3. Input as normal and defining a number `n` and the input gets evenly divided into `n` parts (assuming that each time-period has the same amount of words).

I think option 2 might be the best, as option 1 and 3 might require some preprocessing first.

issue_title: J1939 Bus implementation losing J1939 messages
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Jeff Jasper (Bitbucket: [jajasp1](https://bitbucket.org/jajasp1), GitHub: Unknown)**

---

I noticed I was losing J1939 messages and noticed that there are two threads that remove messages from the queue containing received CAN messages. The first thread is the one from which the bus is iterated over (the client code) and the second being the thread that is created during the instantiation of the Notifier assigned to j1939_notifier.

The messages removed when iterating over the bus are expected. However, the messages removed from the Notifier assigned to j1939_notifier do not get passed on to anything and are lost.

I'll preface the following with the fact that it's not entirely clear what j1939_notifier is for (it appears that it might be a mechanism to send J1939 messages and notify the sender of the response?). A possible client (one that is not interested in sending any messages) code workaround might be to create a istener in the client code and override the listener assigned to j1939_notifier (not sure if this is even possible yet). Doing so would mean all messages would have to be received through the listener and not by iterating over the Bus.

---
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/hardbyte/python-can/issue/15

issue_title: process_exception() should not delete the request when using MIDDLEWARE.
issue_body:
I just discovered today that `CrequestMiddleware.process_response()` should not be deleting the request, due to how exceptions are now handled by the `MIDDLEWARE` setting. Rather than `process_exception()` being called *instead of* `process_response()` when an exception occurs, it gets called before and *in addition to* `process_response()`. 

I discovered this because I have code that attempts to retrieve the current request when a 404 page is rendered, and it was crashing because the request object had already been deleted by the time the `404.html` template was rendered. 

Careful reading of the new MIDDLEWARE docs shows that `process_response()` is *always* called (unless an earlier middleware returns a response). This means it's safe to not delete the request in `process_exception()`, which then allows the request to be retrieved while rendering an error page.

However, I'm fairly sure this *isn't* true up to Django 1.9, or in any newer Django site that uses `MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES` instead of `MIDDLEWARE`. So the right fix here would be to check for `MIDDLEWARE` in `process_exception()`, and to just `pass` if it's defined.

Is the maintainer of this module still watching this issue queue and/or interested in this module? If not, I'd be happy to take over maintainership.

issue_title: Is it possible to increment rate limit on one view and consume on others?
issue_body:
I'm looking to use django-ratelimit to increment a rate limit when a user POSTs to a view, and show a notice if the limit has been exceeded, on either a POST *or* a GET. Is this feature supported?

My goal would be something like so:
```python
from django.views.generic import FormView
from ratelimit.mixins import RatelimitMixin

class ExampleView(RatelimitMixin, FormView):
    ratelimit_key = 'ip'
    ratelimit_method = 'POST'
    ratelimit_rate = '3/m'

```

`PhoneNumber.__init__()` expects a string input, but `PhoneNumberField` passes `valuelist[0]` in directly without any type check, hence the exception.

Either handle the `TypeError` here (raise an `ValueError` instead), or convert `valuelist[0]` to string before constructing the `PhoneNumber` object might fix this issue.

https://github.com/kvesteri/wtforms-alchemy/blob/0.15.0/wtforms_alchemy/fields.py#L513

issue_title: clip.py 1/2 speed of c++ code
issue_body:
I've updated to today's git and ran benchmark of clip.py vs. c++ code.  I got:
numpy << using numpy
2.81051397324
pythran non-vector << this is clip.py
0.938794851303
pythran vector << this is a different pythran version using masks (ignore for now)
1.63022494316
c++ << c++ version
0.424335002899

clip.py:
```python
#pythran export clip(complex128[], float64)
#pythran export clip(complex128[::], float64)
def clip (z, _max):
    out = np.empty (z.shape, dtype=z.dtype)
    for i in range (len(z)):
        if abs(z[i]) > _max:
            out[i] = z[i]/abs(z[i]) * _max
#            out[i] = limit1 (z[i]) * _max
        else:
            out[i] = z[i]

    return out
```
c++ version (inner part of code)
```c++
template<typename T>
inline T clip1 (T x, typename decomplexify<T>::type sat = typename decomplexify<T>::type(1)) {
  if (std::abs (x) > sat)
    return (x / std::abs(x)) * sat;
  else
    return x;
}
```

issue_title: Error when lint outfile path does not exist
issue_body:
When specifying outfile with intermediate dirs like
```sh
python setup.py lint --lint-packages my.package --lint-output reports/lint.out
```
the command aborts with an error
```sh
error: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'reports/lint.out'
```
if the dirs in specified file path don't exist.
As a workaround, in each `setup.py` using linting I override the `PylintCommand`:

```python
import os
...
from setuptools_lint.setuptools_command import PylintCommand

class PylintCommandPatched(PylintCommand):
    
    def finalize_options(self):
        if self.lint_output:
            out_dir = os.path.dirname(self.lint_output)
            os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
        PylintCommand.finalize_options(self)

setup(
      ...
      cmdclass={
          'lint': PylintCommandPatched,
      },
      ...
)
```

Maybe it's possible to create missing dirs on outfile path in the `PylintCommand`?

issue_title: How to correctly re-write history?
issue_body:
I have a project where I'm using Sqlalchemy-Continuum to keep incremental "time-machine" style row snapshots of content in a database (I.E. hourly for 24 hours, daily for a month, weekly for forever). As such, I need to be able to truncate old version rows periodically, without breaking everything.

I've implemented this already, based on my understanding of how the row history is structured (and it seems to work!), but I figure I'd ask for clarification that I'm not subtly breaking something.

Basically, the procedure is as follows:

1. For each row with history, accumulate all the history rows for that source row.
2. Remove any rows that have "expired", given the history specifications described above. Delete them from the history table.
3. For the remaining rows, build a sequence of the transaction IDs for each row in the correct (chronological order).
4. Re-link the transaction sequence columns in the version table using the new transaction sequence. Make sure the most recent row has the `end_transaction_id` entry set to `None`.
5. (This is TODO for me). At some point, delete any dead entries from the `transaction` table.

Does this sound reasonable? It seems to work, but I haven't completely read the entire codebase.

issue_title: Issues with BaseTranslationFormSet
duplicating translation in admin.save_model shows blank translations afterwards
issue_body:
Hi,
I noticed an issue in hvad admin (v. 1.8.0)
General usage scenario for me is:
1. we serve documents in 4 languages
2. better serving a copy of one language into another than nothing.
3. I need to show all translations with django rest framework (works perfectly)

(Simplified) model:
```
from django.db import models

from hvad.models import TranslatableModel, TranslatedFields


class BindingDocument(TranslatableModel):
    translations = TranslatedFields(
        document=models.FileField(),
        label=models.CharField(_("Document label"), max_length=255),
        timestamp=models.DateTimeField(auto_created=True, auto_now=True),
    )
```

admin:
```
from django.contrib import admin
from django.conf import settings

from hvad.admin import TranslatableAdmin

from .models import BindingDocument


@admin.register(BindingDocument)
class BindingDocumentAdmin(TranslatableAdmin):
    list_display = ('all_translations',)
    
    def get_queryset(self, request):
        return BindingDocument.objects.prefetch_related('translations')

    def save_model(self, request, obj, form, change):
        super().save_model(request, obj, form, change)
        document = obj.document
        label = obj.label

        for (language_code, _) in settings.LANGUAGES:
            if obj.translations.filter(language_code=language_code).exists():
                continue
            obj.translate(language_code)
            obj.document = document
            obj.label = label
            obj.save()
```

Database is filled correctly (i.e. one translation per language is created, all contain same data), rest framework (using `TranslationsMixin`) also works perfectly. 
Admin shows all languages in list view, as expected. However when going to any detail page other than the language in which translation was created, the result is blank. When entering content in blank fields, I get a `duplicate key value violates unique constraint "products_bindingdocument_language_code_master_id_add98726_uniq`exception.

My guess is that it has something to do with cache internals. Looks like a bug to me, perhaps it has a workaround?

issue_title: Serializer parameter `depth` should not restrict embedded documents
issue_body:
Rationale:
1. The restriction is to avoid recursive circular references, embedded documents can not have such recursion and the restriction makes no sense
2. At bottom of depth, the reference field is easily serialized as an id-field. For embedded doc there is no meaningful replacement, it comes just as loss of data.

issue_title: The Table.validate_and_ methods silently drop fields set to None
issue_body:
In [`Table._validate_fields()`](https://github.com/web2py/pydal/blob/32cde26be1913c67738f21f1a674f5dadfe72a8a/pydal/objects.py#L752), we have:

        elif value is not None:
            new_fields[fieldname] = value

`._validate_fields` is called by `.validate_and_insert` and `.validate_and_update`. It returns `new_fields`, which then gets passed to the `.insert()` or `.update()` method. If you explicitly set a field to `None`, that field will be excluded from `new_fields` and therefore excluded from the actual insert or update. This can cause two problems:

1. It is not possible to update a field to have a value of `None` (i.e., `db.mytable.validate_and_update(record_id, myfield=None)` will have no effect).
2. For inserts or updates, if a field needed by a computed field is explicitly set to `None`, the computation will fail (even if `None` would have been a valid value within the computation). For example, if you have `db.mytable.validate_or_insert(needed_by_computed_field=None)`, the computed field that relies on `needed_for_computed_field` will not be set.

I assume the reason for the condition in the `elif` above is to drop fields that were not in the original set of fields and also have no default, but simply checking for `None` is not a sufficient test because it also excludes cases in which the value is explicitly set to `None`, which is a valid value.

issue_title: Allow work with encoding different to utf 8
issue_body:
I have an application that uses a SQLServer database, which is part of another system. The database is latin1

when I create the DAL object I pass the parameter db_codec = 'latin1'

This solves the conversion to utf8 when I read data, but when I write it does in utf8.

I solved it by doing the following: If I create a new record from a SQLFORM use "dbio = False", then analyze and convert the dictionary form to latin1:

for k in form.vars:
         if isinstance (form.vars [k], unicode) or isinstance (form.vars [k], str):
             form.vars [k] = form.vars [k] .decode ('utf8'). encode ('latin1')

db.mytable.insert (** dict (form.vars))

Then, for a very simple task you have to write a lot of code.

The proposal is to become the edges to encoding necessary:

Database <---enconding1 (default utf8)---> DAL (utf8) <---encoding2 (default utf8)---> Application

issue_title: Be respectful to other people. (original title: well, I have solved this issue but the crude contributor locked the issue)
baselines break with version bumps
issue_body:
Currently, baselines have no notion of which version of detect-secrets created it. This makes things slightly annoying, because with a major version bump, it could invalidate old baselines, requiring the user to recreate the baseline to be compliant once again.

At the very least, we should have baselines know which detect-secrets version created it, so we can be aware when this happens.

issue_title: Inconsistencies in graph_models with abstract base class models
issue_body:
I'm working on a project with a non-developer friend, and I'm finding that Graphviz is a great communication tool.

Unfortunately, as the level of abstraction in my code increases, a number of quirks in  `graph_models` and the underlying [modelviz.py](https://github.com/django-extensions/django-extensions/blob/master/django_extensions/management/modelviz.py) have come to light, mostly regarding handling of abstract base classes.

I've tried to identify each quirk as specifically as possible, and while each is likely its own "bug," I still think they belong together in one report:

 1. While field inheritance is implemented recursively, model inheritance is not. For example, I have `Profile` and `User` models, both of which inherit from a `TimestampMixin` that itself inherits from a `CreatedTimestampMixin`. The fields from both mixins are correctly included in both `Profile` and `User`; `TimestampMixin` correctly appears as a model; but `CreatedTimestampMixin` is left out of the graph entirely.
 2. When grouping with the `-g` flag, an abstract model is clustered within the "first" app containing a model that inherits from it, rather than being clustered within the app in which it is declared. For instance, the aforementioned `TimestampMixin` is declared in `apputils`, but in the output it is instead included in the "first" app containing a model that inherits from it.
 3. I write "first" in scare quotes, because the order in which apps are output by `graph_models` is currently undefined; back-to-back calls to `graph_models` will often produce different output.

I think it would take a fairly significant restructuring to fix all of these issues; but if it's agreed that such changes would be widely beneficial, then I'm willing to take a stab at implementing them myself.

issue_title: api.content.create replaces archetype-content title with site title during Plone site creation
issue_body:
How to reproduce:
1. Use `plone.api.content.create` to create content in "setuphandlers.py" (or custom GS profile configuration step).
2. Create a new Plone site and select the profile in question to be installed for the new site.

Then

Each new Archetype-based content object created with plone.api.content.create will have site title as its title.

Why
1. plone.api.content.create calls Archetypes' processForm-method
2. processForm-method tries to re-fill content fields from request
3. title-widget finds a title field from the request (because the default form for creating a new Plone site has a title field)

Our local fix is to pop "title" key from the request.form before running code with plone.api.content.create.

But note that this is not limited to the site creation from but any values in request.form may cause similar surprises with api.content.create.

If it's not a feature to read field values for plone.api.content.create from request, then maybe request.form should somehow be cleared before creating Archetypes-content and restored afterwise.

issue_title: test_large_send overflows nbytes()
issue_body:

```

zmq/tests/test_socket.py::TestSocket::test_large_send
```

After some investigation, I found out that an overflow happens in `nbytes`.

After applying the following patch
```diff
diff --git a/zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx b/zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx
index e46ff1b..de5f078 100644
--- a/zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx
+++ b/zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx
@@ -116,7 +116,7 @@ IPC_PATH_MAX_LEN = get_ipc_path_max_len()
 # inline some small socket submethods:
 # true methods frequently cannot be inlined, acc. Cython docs
 
-cdef inline int nbytes(buf):
+cdef inline int nbytes(buf) except -1:
     """get n bytes"""
     if PY_VERSION_HEX >= 0x03030000:
         return buf.nbytes
```

zmq/sugar/socket.py:392: in send
    return super(Socket, self).send(data, flags=flags, copy=copy, track=track)
zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx:729: in zmq.backend.cython.socket.Socket.send

zmq/backend/cython/socket.pyx:786: in zmq.backend.cython.socket.Socket.send

issue_title: Data processing lifecycle of `EDTFField`?
issue_body:
We need to give some thought to the lifecycle of data going into the `EDTFField`.

Initial questions are:

1. when should the EDTF field be updated based on the value of the associated `natural_text_field`?
   1. if the `natural_text_field` is cleared, presumable the EDTF and other derived field values should also be cleared?
   1. if the `natural_text_field` value is invalid or cannot be turned into an EDTF, should the existing EDTF and other derived field values be cleared?
   1. since parsing of `natural_text_field` is very slow, could we somehow cache the before-and-after values and only re-parse it when it changes?

1. can/should we permit direct interaction with the EDTFField?
   1. the field is intended to be used only via automatic processing of the `natural_text_field` source field, but this is both slow and likely to lead to less precise data if there is already a known EDTF value that could be set directly (e.g. via the GLAMkit Collections API)
   1. if the EDTF field is set directly and the `natural_text_field` is not set, should this be reverse-generated from the EDTF value?
   1. what if the EDTF field is set directly, then a `natural_text_field` value is set subsequently. Should the  EDTF value always be replaced by the parsed`natural_text_field` value?
   1. should we permit, and do we need to detect or handle, the other derived fields like `lower_fuzzy_field` being set directly instead of via the EDTFField's processing

issue_title: Client event emitters are not entirely separate
issue_body:
Take this sample code:
```
import botocore.session


def print_something(**kwargs):
    print('foo')


def main():
    session = botocore.session.get_session()

    client1 = session.create_client('s3')
    client2 = session.create_client('s3')
    client1.meta.events.register('after-call.s3.ListBuckets', print_something, 'unique_id')
    client2.meta.events.register('after-call.s3.ListBuckets', print_something, 'unique_id')

    print('Calling client 1:')
    client1.list_buckets()

    print('Calling client 2:')
    client2.list_buckets()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

The expected output would be:
```
Calling client 1:
foo
Calling client 2:
foo
```

However the actual output is:
```
Calling client 1:
foo
Calling client 2:
```

My guess is that ``unique_id`` from ``client1`` is getting picked up in ``client2`` when it should not be because clients are suppose to have separate event emitters.

issue_title: Environment activation on the shebang line?
issue_body:
I was inspired to ask about specifying the project dir as a command line arg (https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/2237) while thinking about invoking tools in their own venvs. At some point I realized that I could use a venv's `python` to invoke a tool and it would just work without having to activate anything my shell. I assume this is entirely by design.

With pipenv managing venv paths, it might be compelling to be able to invoke a tool via pipenv, where pipenv would determine the venv from the tool's path and not the current working directory. Perhaps pipenv could be the tool runner on the shebang line, something like this:

```
#!/usr/bin/env pipenv run

import mystuff
import sys

def main(args):
  # And so forth.
  return 0

if __name__ == '__main__':
  sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
```

Is this compelling? Or am I missing a different best practice for organizing tools with their own venvs? Or do people just prefer to not do this and use global site-packages for everything they intend to run on their own machines? (And if I'm dev'ing my own tools, I would do actual releases into my everyday-carry environment, and use venvs just for development?)

issue_title: RequestSequence is difficult to use on Python 3
issue_body:
RequestSequence is very picky about types of arguments, but lacks any checking or coercion. For example, in the request tuple:

* ``b'get'`` vs. ``'get'``—the former is required
* ``b'get'`` vs. ``'GET'``—the former is required

In the response tuple:

* Headers must be a dict of strings, as noted in #123, which is inconsistent with HasHeaders. HasHeaders requires lists of strings.

And generally speaking, writing out these tuples is easy to botch. Perhaps we should add a builder API to make it easier?

issue_title: Remove inherit dependency and use Object.create for inheritance
issue_body:
Would you be open to removing the module `inherit` and use [`Object.create`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object/create#Classical_inheritance_with_Object.create()) to provide inheritance at this place: https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/blob/master/lib/js-yaml/exception.js#L30
This is the only place `inherit` module is getting used.

Why I am proposing this is because of the license issue which we have seen for `inherit` and because of that we had to pin down version of `js-yaml` in eslint library. 
`inherit` has not been released in over 2 years now so i am not sure when that would happen.
Ref: https://github.com/eslint/eslint/issues/4553

If this is accepted then I will send a PR.

Thanks

CC: @puzrin

issue_title: use scrapyd-deploy is error
Reset offset for unowned partitions in balanced consumer group
issue_body:
I want to be able to make all consumers in a consumer group go "back in time". That is, I have a balanced consumer group that has been running for a while, but I need it to reprocess some range of messages that are present in Kafka as if it hadn't seen them before. (note: this is *not* the same as auto_reset, where you're creating the offsets for the same time.)

The obvious way to do this is:

        # find the partitions that we need to reset
        topic = kafka.topics[topic_name]
        partitions = topic.partitions.values()

        # build a consumer to get access to the consumer group API
        consumer = topic.get_balanced_consumer(consumer_group="consumer_group_name")

        consumer.reset_offsets(
            [(partition, epoch_timestamp)
             for partition in partitions])

However, this throws an exception:

      File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pykafka/balancedconsumer.py", line 671, in reset_offsets
        self._consumer.reset_offsets(partition_offsets=partition_offsets)
      File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py", line 593, in reset_offsets
        raise KafkaException("Unknown partition supplied to reset_offsets\n%s", e)
    pykafka.exceptions.KafkaException: ('Unknown partition supplied to reset_offsets\n%s', KeyError(<pykafka.partition.Partition at 0x7f196afc55d0 (id=0)>,))

That's because SimpleConsumer is trying to ["turn Partitions into their corresponding OwnedPartitions"](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/e18bdeb3586fab21493b755d564d6b4aa8ec7ef2/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L627). But I want to reset the stored offset in Zookeeper for Partitions that I don't own and that other consumers may currently be processing.

It may be okay for me to just take ownership of all of the partitions, reset the offsets, and then relinquish ownership so the other consumers in the group resume from wherever I reset them to. But I don't see a straight forward way to do that in pykafka's API.

**PyKafka version**: 2.1.2
**Kafka version**: 0.8.2

issue_title: Behaviour of linefeed above scrolling region
issue_body:
Assume the scrolling region is set from line 3 to 27 (VT-100 coordinates).
If I send CSI H to bring the cursor to 1, 1, then send a linefeed, I think the cursor should be at 2, 1.
However, it moves to 3, 1.

If I run the following in the shell, with screen:
```
clear;echo -ne '\x1b[3;27r\x1b[H\n';read

Then press CTRL-A i to check the cursor position, it shows column 2, line 1 as I expected.

However, the same sequence with pyte:
```
stream.feed(b'\x1b[3;27r\x1b[H\n')
print(screen.cursor.y, screen.cursor.x)

returns (2, 0) -- line 3 column 1 accounting for the 0-based offsets.

I'm not sure who's correct here, except it looks like this is corrupting my nano display if I run ssh -> screen -> nano.

issue_title: DELETE operation always returns 200 on success, while the spec suggests otherwise
issue_body:
Refs https://github.com/Kinto/kinto-client/pull/66#issuecomment-195251192

>So, it depends which status you're getting back. The HTTP spec says that:
>
>- A 200 means that "The request has succeeded"
>- A 202 means that the request was accepted but not yet fulfilled;
>- A 204 means that "The server has fulfilled the request" but there is nothing to answer (that's what's usually used on DELETE operations)
>
>In cliquet, [it seems that we return a 200](https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/blob/master/cliquet/resource/__init__.py#L499), which doesn't seem to be consistent with what you're describing here.
>
>Please, open an issue about that, yes!

**Problem:**
Travis currently uses mysql version 5.6.33 which has [bug #77591](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/blob/fc32cf2a522f0c464715f9998d1a6cfd11a3eef6/invenio_access/alembic/dbe499bfda43_prevent_null_in_actionusers_for_.py#L38).
This bug prevents [this alembic recipe](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-access/blob/fc32cf2a522f0c464715f9998d1a6cfd11a3eef6/invenio_access/alembic/dbe499bfda43_prevent_null_in_actionusers_for_.py#L38) from running correctly.

**Solution:**
One solution would be to install mysql-5.7 on travis but we would have to use it in every module which is not acceptable given how travis is slow. We will instead change the alembic recipe.

issue_title: JavaMultipleMethod mismatch for Interface arguments
issue_body:
JavaMultipleMethod resolves to a wrong method signature in a case where matching should be done by the interfaces. Consider the following [Cipher.init](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/javax/crypto/Cipher.html):

        Cipher = autoclass("javax.crypto.Cipher")
        SecretKeySpec = autoclass("javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec")

        default_key = [0x00] * 16
        secret_key_spec = SecretKeySpec(default_key, "DESede")

        # The signature we want is (int, java.security.Key) [Key is interface]
        # the signature this resolves is (int, java.security.Certificate)
        # JavaException: Invalid instance of u'javax/crypto/spec/SecretKeySpec' passed for a u'java/security/cert/Certificate'
        cipher.init(Cipher.DECRYPT_MODE, secret_key_spec)
 
A (temporary) solution would be manually pull out the correct underlying call signature from `JavaMultipleMethod` object using the interface name in the signature. However is this possible? I did not find any examples doing this and my poking from console did not yield results.

issue_title: joblib.parallel with patsy?
issue_body:
So one of my datasets can be super-huge and therefore, super-slow by my impatient standards. I can sit there for 3-5 minutes just waiting for a design matrix to be built (on one core). There's lots of terms/factors in this model. I have plenty of spare CPU's just sitting waiting for this to finish before the GLM can be kicked off. 

Crazy idea... would it make any sense to incorporate `joblib.parallel` in patsy? 

I have 2 ideas... 

Simple idea is to just call `joblib.parallel` on the `dmatrix` call and make those in parallel. 

Slightly more crazy idea would be to try to incorporate this inside `patsy`. I see 2 potential (somewhat) easy placements...

[here](https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/master/patsy/build.py#L442) and [here](https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/master/patsy/build.py#L886)

I recognize this brings opens a can of worms--transferring the data and state between the parent and child nodes is not free... new complexity in debugging... And doing things in parallel should be after other optimizations. (Tell me... is 5 minutes to run dmatrix on 30 million rows with about 50 output columns slower than you'd think?)

So before I go down this potentially very dumb rabbit hole, I'm happy to hear the thoughts of people who have better knowledge/experience with these topics.

issue_title: Upgrading postgres steps
issue_body:
I have a patroni running with a single postgres 9.5.4. I want to upgrade postgres to the latest 10.1. But I am not able to find any instructions regarding this. My `.yml` file is attached. (Renamed it to `.txt` for upload to succeed)
[postgres.txt](https://github.com/zalando/patroni/files/1473985/postgres.txt)

I have followed the following steps.

1. Stopped `patroni`
2. Installed `postgressql-10` by doing 
   
```
sudo add-apt-repository "deb http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt/ trusty-pgdg main"
wget --quiet -O - https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install postgresql-10
```
3. Stopped both the postgres server

```
sudo service postgresql stop
```

4. Created a new data directory for `postgressql-10` and did `initdb`
```
sudo -H -u postgres mkdir -p /var/lib/postgresql/10/data/postgresql0
sudo -H -u postgres /usr/lib/postgresql/10/bin/pg_ctl -D /var/lib/postgresql/10/data/postgresql0 initdb -o '--data-checksums'
```

```
cd /tmp
sudo -H -u postgres /usr/lib/postgresql/10/bin/pg_upgrade \
   -b /usr/lib/postgresql/9.5/bin \
   -B /usr/lib/postgresql/10/bin \

issue_title: [makeInstancesUFO] move to ufoProcessor?
issue_body:
The designspace reader/writer in `mutatorMath.ufo` has been succeeded by [ufoProcessor](https://github.com/LettError/ufoProcessor) and [fontTools.designspaceLib](https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/tree/master/Lib/fontTools/designspaceLib). 

* `designspaceLib` is the reader and writer
* `ufoProcessor` is a subclass that can generate UFOs with both mutatorMath and varlib.model if necessary. The varlib.model math has some advantages with non-aligned masters, but it can not extrapolate. 

The designspace format has been upped to `4` to reflect some changes in the rule element. I have no plans to update `mutatorMath.ufo`.

issue_title: Expose scss file in "style" field of package.json
issue_body:
Hello,

I am trying use `bootstrap-sass` via `npm`, `node-sass` and the `sass-module-importer`. The importer [documents](https://www.npmjs.com/package/sass-module-importer) this lookup strategy:

    1. Set a SCSS/Sass/CSS file on the "main" field of their package.json/bower.json
    2. Set a SCSS/Sass/CSS file on the "style" field of their package.json/bower.json
    3. Have a index.css file on the root of their module

If I look at the `bootstrap` npm package, [it uses](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/7c86bc9664ddc3a1965fe91d7a0db97eb9348448/package.json#L21) the `style` field in `package.json`. It seems [this field is becoming a de facto standard](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/32037150/style-field-in-package-json).

Is it possible to get a similar setup and expose the main scss file in the `style` field of `package.json`?

issue_title: JarRules(pattern, Duplicate.CONCAT) /jar-tool needs to handle missing newlines at end of input
issue_body:
I am using 0.0.48-square-20150921-01

We use fat jars for deployment.

I have a binary that is including `com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.19` and `com.sun.jersey.contribs:jersey-multipart:1.19`

Both jars contain the file `META-INF/services/javax.ws.rs.ext.MessageBodyReader`

```
cat ~/.ivy2/pants/com.sun.jersey.contribs/jersey-multipart/jars/t/META-INF/services/javax.ws.rs.ext.MessageBodyReader
com.sun.jersey.multipart.impl.MultiPartReaderClientSide
com.sun.jersey.multipart.impl.MultiPartReaderServerSide
```

```
$ cat ~/ivy2/pants/com.sun.jersey/jersey-core/jars/t/META-INF/services/javax.ws.rs.ext.MessageBodyReader 
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.StringProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.ByteArrayProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.FileProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.InputStreamProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.DataSourceProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.RenderedImageProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.MimeMultipartProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.FormProvider
... a bunch more ...
```

But neither one of these files contain newlines at the end.  

```
od -xa javax.ws.rs.ext.MessageBodyReader 
0000000      6f63    2e6d    7573    2e6e    656a    7372    7965    6d2e
           c   o   m   .   s   u   n   .   j   e   r   s   e   y   .   m
0000020      6c75    6974    6170    7472    692e    706d    2e6c    754d
           u   l   t   i   p   a   r   t   .   i   m   p   l   .   M   u
0000040      746c    5069    7261    5274    6165    6564    4372    696c
           l   t   i   P   a   r   t   R   e   a   d   e   r   C   l   i
0000060      6e65    5374    6469    0a65    6f63    2e6d    7573    2e6e
           e   n   t   S   i   d   e  nl   c   o   m   .   s   u   n   .
0000100      656a    7372    7965    6d2e    6c75    6974    6170    7472
           j   e   r   s   e   y   .   m   u   l   t   i   p   a   r   t
0000120      692e    706d    2e6c    754d    746c    5069    7261    5274
           .   i   m   p   l   .   M   u   l   t   i   P   a   r   t   R
0000140      6165    6564    5372    7265    6576    5372    6469    0065
           e   a   d   e   r   S   e   r   v   e   r   S   i   d   e    
0000157
```

The concatenated file in our fat jars looks like:

```
cat META-INF/services/javax.ws.rs.ext.MessageBodyReader 
com.sun.jersey.multipart.impl.MultiPartReaderClientSide
com.sun.jersey.multipart.impl.MultiPartReaderServerSidecom.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.StringProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.ByteArrayProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.FileProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.InputStreamProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.DataSourceProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.RenderedImageProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.MimeMultipartProvider
com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.FormProvider
... a bunch more...
```

Which means that both classes `com.sun.jersey.multipart.impl.MultiPartReaderServerSide` and `com.sun.jersey.core.impl.provider.entity.StringProvider` are no longer configured for ServiceLoader  (http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/ServiceLoader.html)

I'm not exactly sure what the best way to fix this is.  

1) If these were two binary files that needed to be concatenated, adding in a newline would not be a good strategy.   Maybe we need a CONCAT_TEXT rule?
2)  Maybe jar-tool's CONCAT strategy is too generic - are there other uses of this strategy besides concatenating META-INF/services/ files?  We could just use CONCAT to always mean text files and add support for a CONCAT_BINARY rule if there is a use case for concatenating binary files.

issue_title: PytestRun --fast multi-source-chroots do not account for namespace packages
issue_body:
A classic case here is a repo layout of:
```
src/python/lib/item.py
tests/python/lib/test_item.py
```

Here we have tests with a parallel package structure to the code under test, but separated into two source roots. Under the covers, we construct an execution pex by PEX_PATH combining two independent source pexes, neither of which is declaring namespace packages for `lib` in this example - and this leads to an inability to find code.

issue_title: XSS vulnerability
issue_body:
Hi, first of all, thanks for this project; it saved me a lot of time.
I've just discovered that ouput from database is not sanitized berfore rendering it in html, and as far as I know, this makes this application vulnerable to XSS attacks.
It would be nice that EXPLORER_TRANSFORMS could skip the html sanitization, but documentation should clarify that it's use with char fields can be dangerous.

issue_title: Gauge object does not have labels method
issue_body:
```
>>> import prometheus_client
>>> x = prometheus_client.Counter('foo', 'foobar')
>>> x.labels
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: 'Counter' object has no attribute 'labels'
```

The documentation seems to imply that this is possible. This is on 0.2.0

Relevant README docs:

Labels
All metrics can have labels, allowing grouping of related time series.

See the best practices on naming and labels.

Taking a counter as an example:

```
from prometheus_client import Counter
c = Counter('my_requests_total', 'HTTP Failures', ['method', 'endpoint'])
c.labels('get', '/').inc()
c.labels('post', '/submit').inc()
```

issue_body:
When the content db schema is updated, the `migrate` command doesn't affected downloaded databases. However, the app still assumes that that all databases are compatible.

This results in DB errors when simply trying to browse the app. For example, after upgrading kolibri, the learn app would not load and simply returned

```
no such column: content_license.license_description
```

Even if the goal was to somehow instruct users that they need to re-import content, the current behavior makes that difficult because the UI becomes ostensibly unavailable. (Technically, you can manually enter the 'management' URL but most users will not do that)

Things we could do:

* add schema version checking during content DB loading, and skip any that don't conform to the current schema
* load DB meta-data for out-of-date DBs, but surface user-feedback that they are no longer available because they're not compatible, and provide a quick link to re-import them
* add the ability to migrate content DB schemas after kolibri is updated

issue_title: DatabaseWrapper has no data_types
issue_body:
Have an issue using django-pyodbc 1.0.0 with Django 1.8.15.

When saving (inserting) an object to a pyodbc database I get a `TypeError` when pyodbc tries to get the `pk_db_type` (compiler.py line 533):

```python
# mangle SQL to return ID from insert
# http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms177564.aspx
if self.return_id and self.connection.features.can_return_id_from_insert:
    col = self.connection.ops.quote_name(meta.pk.db_column or meta.pk.get_attname())
    # Determine datatype for use with the table variable that will return the inserted ID
    pk_db_type = _re_data_type_terminator.split(meta.pk.db_type(self.connection))[0]
```

Problem is that the `django.db.models.fields.Field.db_type` method in Django 1.8.15 tries to get the data_type from a different place than Django 1.7 did:

```python
# Django 1.7
data = DictWrapper(self.__dict__, connection.ops.quote_name, "qn_")
try:
    return connection.creation.data_types[self.get_internal_type()] % data
except KeyError:
    return None

# Django 1.8
data = DictWrapper(self.__dict__, connection.ops.quote_name, "qn_")
try:
    return connection.data_types[self.get_internal_type()] % data
except KeyError:
    return None
```

Looking at pyodbc's code I see there is a data_types attribute on the DatabaseCreation model, but not on the DatabaseWrapper, whereas it seems to be the other way around with Django's built in DB engines. So this call fails as `connection.data_types` doesn't have any types in it, returns `None` and the regexp split fails as it's not a string.

Hope that makes sense, I don't know too much about django-pyodbc/Django database engine internals apart from what I discovered today so I apologise if I have misunderstood anything here.

issue_title: Inconsistent partial error handling with Ads Py Google
issue_body:
One difference between Google Ads and Ads Py Google is when sending a mutate operation with the “partial failure” (https://github.com/googleads/googleads-python-lib/blob/master/googleads/adwords.py L190) option set to true. In Ads Py Google, the returned Python dictionary would have the key “value” which is a list of all the successful mutated entities. Any unsuccessful mutated entities are omitted from this list, and included in the list keyed by “partialFailureErrors”. In Google Ads, the returned Python Suds object has the attribute “value”, which returns a list of the successful mutated entities, but includes an empty “suds.sax.text.Text” field as a placeholder for where the entities that did not mutate successfully occurred. Basically in Ads Py Google, they were omitted from “value”, but in Google Ads they have a placeholder in “value”.
 
Are you guys aware of this difference? I am curious if this is expected behavior, and if it is something that we should expect going forward.

issue_title: charm build includes trusty dir when building in "."
issue_body:
If you don't set a `JUJU_REPOSITORY` and leave charm build to build in the current layer dir (`.`), then it picks up previous `trusty` dir, and then embeds it inside the built charm (inside the previously generated `trusty` dir), leading to an every growing embedded Inception-esque tree of trusty's, e.g. for the charm I'm working on I had 6 levels deep since my last `rm -rf` of the build dirs:

```bash
$ ls -lah trusty/errbot/trusty/errbot/trusty/errbot/trusty/errbot/trusty/errbot/
total 144K
drwxrwxr-x 9 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 .
drwxrwxr-x 3 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 ..
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  66K Feb 12 11:09 .build.manifest
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes 4.8K Feb 12 11:12 config.yaml
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  265 Feb 12 11:12 copyright
drwxrwxr-x 4 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 deps
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes   19 Jan 21 11:09 .gitignore
drwxrwxr-x 3 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 hooks
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  112 Feb 12 11:12 layer.yaml
drwxrwxr-x 3 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 lib
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  501 Feb 12 11:12 Makefile
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  452 Feb 12 11:01 metadata.yaml
drwxrwxr-x 2 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 reactive
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  450 Feb 10 22:16 README.md
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes   14 Feb 12 11:12 requirements.txt
drwxrwxr-x 2 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 templates
drwxrwxr-x 2 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 tests
-rw-rw-r-- 1 wes wes  111 Feb 12 11:12 tox.ini
drwxrwxr-x 2 wes wes 4.0K Feb 12 11:12 wheelhouse
```

The series directory should ideally be blacklisted when building into "." (which takes care of the edge-cases where people include their own trusty/xenail/etc dir, they would **need** to define `JUJU_REPOSITORY` for it to work anyway).

issue_title: Add L2VPN/EVPN to next-hop sizing check to fix L2VPN/EVPN peers
issue_body:
<!---
Verify first that your issue/request is not already reported on GitHub.
Also test if the latest release, and master branch are affected too.
-->

##### ISSUE TYPE
<!--- Pick one below and delete the rest: -->
 - Bug Report


##### SUMMARY
<!--- Explain the problem briefly -->
Commit 4aed9cbf81cbe544253e7da7c97feeb238bfea43 added next-hop sizing checks but did not add the sizing information for L2VPN / EVPN address-family.  This causes L2VPN/EVPN peers to be torn down.

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
Connect to a L2VPN/EVPN neighbor and watch the session come down.


##### FIX

I corrected this with just a quick add to the Family class - but given the rest of the changes in the commit there may be other areas that need some attention for EVPN.

Without this - an EVPN peer gets torn down (with an unsupported message being sent) , after this adjustment the peer is stable and passing information.

diff --git a/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py b/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py
index 62b90a3..2747811 100644
--- a/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py
+++ b/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py
@@ -264,6 +264,7 @@ class Family (object):
                (AFI.ipv6,SAFI.flow_ip):   ((0,16,32), 0),
                (AFI.ipv6,SAFI.flow_vpn):  ((0,16,32), 0),
                (AFI.l2vpn,SAFI.vpls):     ((4,),      0),
+               (AFI.l2vpn,SAFI.evpn):     ((4,),      0),
                (AFI.bgpls,SAFI.bgp_ls):   ((4,),      0),
        }

(for small items like this I just copy n paste - but if a full merge req. is preferred - just let me know)

I believe this due to the:

https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/27779

I was unable to reproduce this issue on different dockers than ```1.12.6, build 1398f24/1.12.6```. On this docker following code is sufficient:

import docker
cli = docker.from_env(version='auto')
cli.containers.run(image="busybox:latest", command="ls")

  File "/tmp/tmp.oszXxbDvRO/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/api/client.py", line 341, in _multiplexed_response_stream_helper
    header = response.raw.read(STREAM_HEADER_SIZE_BYTES)
  File "/tmp/tmp.oszXxbDvRO/lib/python2.7/site-packages/urllib3/response.py", line 384, in read

issue_title: Document "max_pages" and "max_items" on "pages" method.
Metadata `public_method_name` is confusing.
issue_body:
We have a metadata item `public_method_name` which overrides linked names in documentation.

For example, say a method refers to fetch(), but we have a "Fancy Fetch" wrapper and made generated method `_fetch` private. We want documentation to refer to the public one, not the one originally referred to in the extracted metadata.

The name `public_method_name` is misleading and easily confused with `python_name` which is the name to be used in the generated code for the generated method.

issue_title: Trinity does not limit total peer connections
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Now that we are *useful* and staying connected to peers, a long running trinity node will acquire a **lot** of peer connections inbound, eventually swamping the node's connection.


### How can it be fixed

Finish https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/824

issue_title: Deduplication of `Message.gas_price` and `Message.origin`
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The `Message.gas_price` and `Message.origin` are copied for every level of computation during VM execution, despite them remaining *constant* for the entire context of the VM execution.

It would be nice to remove this duplication in favor of having these values stored in *one* location.

Also see: `sighash` from https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/259/files


### How can it be fixed

Not sure.

Best idea after thinking about this for only a few minutes is to introduce a `ComputationContext` object of some sort that is passed into the outermost `Computation` class, and then to pass that object down through all of the child computations.  This would eliminate copying these values in favor of giving each `Computation` instance a reference to this singular object instance.

issue_title: Figure out what to do with `web3.shh`, `web3.bzz`, etc.
issue_body:
### What is wrong

Right now we have some in-flight functionality for Whisper V6: #117 

As it stands, we *can* write in support for this in `web3.shh` but I want to figure out a future proof way to do this.  I see the following problems coming down the road.

- API differences between nodes (parity, geth, ...)
- API differences across protocol versions (v5, v6, v???)

### How it could be fixed.

#### When you have middleware, everything looks like a middleware...??

Dealing with API differences across different nodes is an issue that exists in the `eth_` namespace as well.  Currently, we can mostly address these with middlewares, or intentionally not address them but raise helpful errors.  

#### Namespace by node

Alternatively we **could** implement submodules like `w3.geth.shh` and `w3.parity.shh` but that adds some development and testing overhead.

#### Namespace by version

For dealing with actual changes between protocol versions... not clear.  Versioning the actual module might make sense, i.e. `web3.shh.v6.WhisperModule`.  Still poses some usability hurdles.

#### Separate package

We could move `web3.shh` to it's own package `web3-shh` and require installation of the separate package and manual addition of the `shh` module.

issue_title: Add ability to change destination for underscore js media
Disallow sending arbitrary options to ACE
issue_body:
There are some options to ACE that are useful, such as `-n` and `--timeout`, but some can break PyDelphin's ability to interpret the results. The useful options could be whitelisted, or they could be made into Python parameters (as is done with `tsdbinfo`). The latter is easier to validate. Both of these options would require PyDelphin to remain relatively in-sync with recent ACE versions.

Perhaps a compromise is to allow arbitrary options on AceProcess, then restrict them for AceParser, AceTransferer, and AceGenerator. This also makes sense because some options are only relevant for certain tasks, like `--show-realization-mrses`.

issue_title: unable to build complex packages after migration : absolute installation path hardcoded during install
issue_body:
It seems that both Anaconda and Miniconda has hard-coded the absolute installation path into many places during install. After I copy the entire Anaconda/Miniconda folder into a new location, existing packages can still run, but new complex packages will encounter compilation error, i.e., a lot of "path not found".

Steps to reproduce the bug:
1. Use a freshly installed copy of Anaconda/Miniconda to install https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq ,  everything goes successfully.
2. Install a freshly installed copy of Anaconda/Miniconda, copy the entire folder into a different location, then use the conda in that new location (must set PATH, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, etc. correctly) to install https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq , the compilation fails with many "library not found" errors.

I hope the developer can improve the relocation flexibility of the software. Thanks!

issue_title: HTTP 503 error when local http channel is queried after other channels
issue_body:
I've been receiving strange 503 errors when attempting to use simple conda commands (conda search) with multiple local channels configured. One is a "file" channel, the other a simple local http server. 

The channels, when used independently, work fine. 

The conda commands seem to hang, then fail, in the "session.get" in fetch.py's fetch_repodata. 

Observations: if I purposely order the requests such that our local http server comes first, it works fine. If ordered such that the http server request is not first, it fails. This is consistent with or without proxy, with or without cache=True, and with or without enabling concurrency in fetch_index. Also, when the operations fail, I see no indication that any requests even reach the webserver, so it seems to be a client-side failure.

It is always when the local http channel comes after some other channel (even defaults).

issue_title: Row level types (numpy.ndarray 0d)
issue_body:
Hi,

While running some tests I stumbled upon something unexpected to me, bcolz ctable containing different data types for items that should have the same data type (code copied and modified from Bcolz tests).
Some objects are of the type numpy.ndarray while all objects inside the same column should have the same data type.
Is this issue known if yes is this a bcolz or numpy problem or am I misunderstanding something?

I tried in different machines and all of them had the same issue.

Could be this issue related to https://github.com/Blosc/bcolz/issues/21 or are they completely independent?

Can somebody else reproduce this issue (code below), summary of the output object types as comments.

```python
import numpy as np
import bcolz
from collections import defaultdict

def check_types(col):
    check = defaultdict(int)
    for i in range(len(col)):
        val = col[i]
        check[str(type(val))] += 1
    return dict(check)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    ra = np.fromiter(((i, i * 2., i * 3)
                      for i in range(500000)), dtype='i4,f8,i8')
    t = bcolz.ctable(ra)

    print(check_types(t['f0']))
    # {"<type 'numpy.ndarray'>": 401407, "<type 'int'>": 98593}
    print(check_types(t['f1']))
    # {"<type 'float'>": 37153, "<type 'numpy.ndarray'>": 462847}
    print(check_types(t['f2']))
    # {"<type 'numpy.ndarray'>": 462847, "<type 'int'>": 37153}
```

Thank you guys for the great work you are doing in this project.

issue_title: run_transaction temporary compatibilyt
issue_body:
I understand the motivation behind the conflict handling and the need to use a callback, but in existing projects this is going to take a lot of work before being able to switch from PG to CRDB.

Are there any plans to add a backwards compatibility mode where the conflicts are not treated, but instead a warning is raised? This way the migrations from PG to CRDB could be done progressively.

issue_title: ChoicesEnum isn't hashable
issue_body:
* Choices Enum version: 0.1.6
* Python version: 3.6.2
* Operating System: Linux

### Description

I'm porting existing Django code to use choicesenum. One existing use case that doesn't work is to use enum values as keys in a dict, because ChoicesEnum isn't hashable, like the stdlib enum implementation is.

### What I Did

``` python
>>> from enum import Enum, IntEnum
>>> from choicesenum import ChoicesEnum
>>> class Foo(Enum):
...     A = 1
...     B = 2
...     C = 3
...
>>> class Bar(IntEnum):
...     A = 1
...     B = 2
...     C = 3
...
>>> class Baz(ChoicesEnum):
...     A = 1
...     B = 2
...     C = 3
...
>>> Foo(1)
<Foo.A: 1>
>>> hash(Foo(1))
-8133611313320584089
>>> Bar(1)
<Bar.A: 1>
>>> hash(Bar(1))
1
>>> Baz(1)
Baz(1).A
>>> hash(Baz(1))                                                                                         
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: unhashable type: 'Baz'
```

issue_title: [VM] [Feature Request] Add an SSH helper command
issue_body:
It would be cool if there were an `az vm ssh` command that allowed you to specify a VM name, and have it automatically determine the public IP and admin username, and then establish an SSH connection for you. Other CLIs have an equivalent (e.g. [Docker Machine](https://docs.docker.com/machine/reference/ssh/), Minikube), and while it isn't difficult to get a VMs PIP/admin username manually, this workflow helps save a little bit of time and feels more efficient.

`az vm ssh -n <name> -g <group> [-c command]`.

This would either assume you have the necessary private keys installed on the machine, or prompt  you for a password.

issue_title: [Monitor] alert-rule create exposes 'alert-rule-resource-name' and 'name'
issue_body:
### Description
```
Command
    az monitor alert-rules create: Creates or updates an alert rule.

Arguments
    --alert-rule-resource-name [Required]: The name of the alert rule.
    --condition                [Required]: JSON encoded condition configuration. Use @{file} to load
                                           from a file.
    --is-enabled               [Required]: The flag that indicates whether the alert rule is
                                           enabled.
    --name -n                  [Required]: The name of the rule.
    --actions                            : JSON encoded array of actions that are performed when the
                                           alert rule becomes active, and when an alert condition is
                                           resolved. Use @{file} to load from a file.
    --description                        : The description of the alert rule that will be included
                                           in the alert email.
    --location -l                        : Location. You can configure the default location using
                                           `az configure --defaults location=<location>`.  Default:
                                           westus.
    --resource-group -g                  : Name of resource group. You can configure the default
                                           group using `az configure --defaults group=<name>`.
                                           Default: tjp-vm.
    --tags                               : Resource tags.
```

This exposes two required parameters: `--alert-rule-resource-name` and `--name`.  Both refer to the same thing and NEITHER refer to the name or ID of the target resource (which is actually buried deep inside the condition object--FUN TIMES).

issue_title: az storage entity query: error: 'NoneType' object is not iterable
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
With azure-cli 2.0.32, az storage entity query no longer works correctly.

**To Reproduce**
From bash run (where capitalized strings are the appropriate values):
docker run -it microsoft/azure-cli
az storage entity query --account-name ACCOUNT_NAME --account-key ACCOUNT_KEY --table-name TABLE_NAME

The azure cli will error saying "az storage entity query: error: 'NoneType' object is not iterable" (regardless of whether the specified table exists)

**Expected behavior**
If the table exists, this should result in a description of the contents of the table being returned. If not, it should result in an error saying that the table does not exist.

**Environment summary**
Repros on Ubuntu/Debian with version 2.0.32 of azure-cli.

**Additional context**
This command works as expected with azure-cli 2.0.31. Version 2.0.32 appears to have introduced a regression.
