issue_title: match_info missing in cloned request
issue_body:
## Long story short

I am cloning the given request object when there is a special header `X-Method-Override` so that my users can bypass some overly-restrictive firewalls that doesn't allow HTTP methods other than GET/POST.

To implement this feature, I wrote a middleware like this:

```python
async def api_middleware_factory(app, handler):
    async def api_middleware_handler(request):
        method_override = request.headers.get('X-Method-Override', None)
        if method_override:
            request = request.clone(method=method_override)
        if request.match_info.http_exception is not None:
            return request.match_info.http_exception
        return (await handler(request))
    return api_middleware_handler
```

And I have a test case like below, using pytest-aiohttp plugin:

```python
async def test_api_method_override(test_client):
    inner_request = None
    app = web.Application()

    async def dummy_handler(request):
        nonlocal inner_request
        inner_request = request
        return web.Response(body=b'test')

    app.router.add_post('/v{version:\d+}/version', dummy_handler)
    app.middlewares.append(api_middleware_factory)
    client = await test_client(app)

    resp = await client.post('/v2/version', headers={
        'X-Method-Override': 'REPORT',
    })
    assert resp.status == 200
    assert inner_request.method == 'REPORT'
```

## Expected behaviour

The test case passes: `match_info` is cloned as well, so checking `request.match_info.http_exception` should be fine.

## Actual behaviour

The test case fails with:
```
Error handling request
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/joongi/Projects/Lablup/backend.ai-manager/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/web_protocol.py", line 422, in start
    resp = yield from self._request_handler(request)
  File "/Users/joongi/Projects/Lablup/backend.ai-manager/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/web.py", line 306, in _handle
    resp = yield from handler(request)
  File "/Users/joongi/Projects/Lablup/backend.ai-manager/ai/backend/gateway/server.py", line 73, in api_middleware_handler
    if request.match_info.http_exception is not None:
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'http_exception'
```
It seems that `match_info` is missing in the cloned request.

I also found [a suspective code of aiohttp](http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/aiohttp/web_middlewares.html?highlight=match_info) which explicitly retry resolving of the request object after cloning and assigning the private member `_match_info` of the cloned request with the new resolve result. Is this the only way for my use-case or am I missing something?

## Steps to reproduce

Simplified sample codes are given above.

## Your environment

macOS 10.13, Python 3.6.3 via homebrew + pyenv, aiohttp 2.2.5, pytest-aiohttp 0.1.3

issue_title: Add API that will allow/deny/warn automatic decoding of json based on content-type.
issue_body:
Now, aiohttp issue warning if `await response.json()` called and response does not contain correct content-type.
This should be configurable. I suggest to to require `content_type.startswith('application/json')`. Aiohttp's users must be able to turn this check off and also specify his own MIME-type that should pass this check. I understand, that many broken HTTP-servers give JSON-response without setting correct content-type. I think, we should fight against these servers.

To debug `selenium` tests I am trying to use a proxy to record all request/responses communications. In particular, I am using `browsermob-proxy` (see [here](https://github.com/lightbody/browsermob-proxy)) which is running e.g. on port 8080. 

To recreate this error you have to have `browsermobproxy` running on port 8080 (checkout the bin package from [here](https://github.com/lightbody/browsermob-proxy/releases) and start the binary with `browsermob-proxy --port 8080 &`), some unimportant `ipynb` notebook (just as example) and the following python test script:

import time

from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait

# Define the Client to the running browsermob-proxy

# Create the firefox webdriver
profile = webdriver.FirefoxProfile()
selenium_proxy = proxy.selenium_proxy()
profile.set_proxy(selenium_proxy) # SETTING PROXY

issue_title: Deprecate Broker.restream

`Broker.restream` is just a pass through to `Broker.get_documents`. We should deprecate it for simplicity.

Also, these are equivalent:

```python
Broker.process(header, cb)
list(map(cb, Broker.get_documents(header)))
list(map(cb, header.documents()))
```

issue_title: Custom styles should be able to use the application-package-names setting
issue_body:
Currently, `checker.py` uses the `application-package-names` option only if the name of the style matches a whitelist: `['appnexus', 'edited']`:
https://github.com/PyCQA/flake8-import-order/blob/35434d29ddc9c1a580ae0c9ff14b8f00a288fd99/flake8_import_order/checker.py#L44

That means that it is not possible to create a custom style that extends the behaviour of the `AppNexus` or `Edited` styles (ie. that uses `application-package-names` option) unless that style also uses the same name.

I would suggest that instead of comparing the name, a property be added the base `Style` class like `accepts_application_package_names` which is set to `False` in the `Style` class but set to `True` in the `AppNexus` and `Edited` classes. Then the logic in `checker.py` should be changed to check for this flag in the style class.

issue_title: Sshuttle not working over OpenVPN Tunnel
issue_body:
My scenario: I'm running OSX El Capitan, and sometimes work remotely and need to connect to my company's OpenVPN server in order to hook into ACLs and firewalls set up by our customers - this server is set up to route all traffic over the VPN.

I also need to use sshuttle to connect to remote networks behind bastion hosts in our customers' infrastructure.

When using sshuttle and OpenVPN individually, everything works as intended, however when using sshuttle after connecting to an OpenVPN tunnel, the firewall proxy rules don't take effect.

I'm aware that this could be due to OpenVPN _or_ sshuttle, however colleagues running Linux can connect with no problem, so I suspect this is due to sshuttle using pf rather than iptables on Mac.

Can anyone help resolve this? Happy to provide more detail if necessary.

issue_body:
When using this function gss_inquire_attrs_for_mech() is called.
This function can legally return GSS_S_COMPLETE with GSS_NO_OID_SET for both return arguments.
However we do not catch this case and try to build sets for both causing a seggault as soon as we try to check for mech_set.count inside of c_create_oid_set()

You can test this with MIT (any version) and GSS-NTLMSSP and this snippet:
```
import gssapi

reset_mech = gssapi.OID.from_int_seq("1.3.6.1.4.1.7165.655.1.3")
gssapi.raw.indicate_mechs_by_attrs([reset_mech], None, None)
```

issue_title: Question about double call consume when apply an offer
issue_body:
Hi, I just wondering about how to apply offer for consume offer for django oscar 1.6.4,

I just digging in to the code base, I found out that they call consume 2 time from apply benefit level and line.discount level

for example
* In `apps.basket.abstract_models.py`
```
 def discount(self, discount_value, affected_quantity, incl_tax=True,
                 offer=None):
        """
        Apply a discount to this line
        """
        if incl_tax:
            if self._discount_excl_tax > 0:
                raise RuntimeError(
                    "Attempting to discount the tax-inclusive price of a line "
                    "when tax-exclusive discounts are already applied")
            self._discount_incl_tax += discount_value
        else:
            if self._discount_incl_tax > 0:
                raise RuntimeError(
                    "Attempting to discount the tax-exclusive price of a line "
                    "when tax-inclusive discounts are already applied")
            self._discount_excl_tax += discount_value
        self.consume(affected_quantity, offer=offer)
```

if you see this method above, it call consume in the `def discount method`

* In `apps.offer.conditions.py` in each condition, they have ` def consume_items(self, offer, basket, affected_lines):` and call `line.consume(quantity_to_consume, offer=offer)` method again.

I dont sure it suppose to double call consume like this, In django oscar 1.5.4 version, they are not consume in `def discount` method in `AbstractLine` model

### Technical details
* Oscar version:  django oscar 1.6.4

issue_title: HttpClient.Post sends separate TCP packets for Header and Body
suggestion - version the certificates with the directory, not the filename
issue_body:
I'm in the process of a server migration, and only need to move over the latest certs

this is a bit more work that it could be, because the versioning is:

* archive/example.com/cert1.pem
* archive/example.com/cert2.pem
* archive/example.com/cert3.pem
* archive/example.com/cert4.pem
* archive/example.com/cert5.pem

and the links are:
   cert.pem -> ../../archive/example.com/cert5.pem

that is for each domain x 4 (cert, chain, fullchain, privkey)

my suggestion is to use versioning in buckets:

* archive/example.com/0005/cert.pem
* cert.pem -> ../../archive/example.com/0005/cert.pem

this would allow people to just grab a single bucket when moving servers (or cleaning up)

https://gist.github.com/anntzer/e4fafaa4fb1136d6b93d32cba8f29f9f

This is based off 9c7c2f4 so a little bit of work may be needed to rebase this on master.  Also, I stripped out the figure interaction code because I personally prefer having the windows pop out but it should be easy to restore it; I guess at the end of the day the "best" way to handle this is with another option for "%plot" that allows for individual windows.

issue_title: Move embed controllers out of content.models
issue_body:
The `ImageAttachment` and `ImageGallery` models both define embed controllers as nested member classes. We need to move these out of the models file and into `frontend/embeds.py`

To make things cleaner, lets change the `json` method to `to_json` and the `prepare_data` method to `from_json` -- this is to show that we are converting to/from json between the client and the database.

**ImageController.to_json()**

Input:
```py
{
  'image_id': 1,
  'caption': 'This is a test caption',
  'credit': 'This is a test credit'
}
```

Output:
```py
{
  'image': {
    'filename': 'test-image.jpg',
    'title': 'Test image',
    'url': 'images/test-image.jpg',
    'url_medium': 'images/test-image-medium.jpg',
    'url_thumb': 'images/test-image-thumb.jpg',
    'authors': [],
    'width': 500,
    'height': 200
  },
  'caption': 'This is a test caption',
  'credit': 'This is a test credit'
}
```

The nested JOSN for `image` can be generated like so:
```py
import ImageSerializer from dispatch.apps.api.serializers

image = Image.objects.get(pk=image_id)

serializer = ImageSerializer(image)
serializer.data # this is the image JSON

issue_title: Schema generator for data classes
issue_body:
Python 3.7 will come with a new feature called Data Classes (see [PEP 557](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0557/)). It is available for Python 3.6 on PyPI for demonstration purposes.

It looks like this:
```python
@dataclass
class Artist:
    name: str

@dataclass
class Album:
    title: str
    release_date: datetime.date
    artist: List[Artist]
```

You can immediately notice the resemblance with marshmallow:
```python
class ArtistSchema(Schema):
    name = fields.Str()

class AlbumSchema(Schema):
    title = fields.Str()
    release_date = fields.Date()
    artist = fields.Nested(ArtistSchema())
```

I think for most cases there's enough information in the dataclass for marshmallow to figure out the schemas by itself. I admit that the schema might change while the model will stay the same but for most cases, it won't be a problem.

My idea is to leverage on the dataclass to build a Schema automatically for DRY purposes when it makes sense while still getting the features of marshmallow under the hood. What's your opinion on that? Do you have an idea of implementation so it can be both DRY and extensible?

I may be able to work on a PR for this but I want to go in the right direction.

issue_title: Question: what is the `__transient__` attribute?
issue_body:
It seems it is not a standard Python dunder attribute. For what I have seen it is used to exclude some attributes from `__dict__` before serialization.

Why is this used instead of `__getstate__`?

It seems in `save_inst()` you are actually trying to use `__getstate__` first if it exists, and only if it does not, then you look for `__transient__`. However, in `save_reduce()` you are always directly trying to look for this attribute (if protocol version is >= 2). Is this necessary? Couldn't `__getstate__` tried to be used as well first?

Cross reference: https://github.com/irmen/Pyro4/pull/179

issue_title: Setting custom JSON serializer for Resource affect also serialization of resource's schema document
issue_body:
When I use  custom serializer CamelCaseJSONSerializer 
from http://django-tastypie.readthedocs.org/en/latest/cookbook.html#camelcase-json-serialization for resource. Example:

```python
class TagResource(ModelResource):
    class Meta:
        queryset = Tag.objects.all()
        resource_name = 'tag'
        serializer = CamelCaseJSONSerializer()
        default_format = 'application/json'
        list_allowed_methods = ['get', 'post']
        detail_allowed_methods = ['get', 'put', 'delete', 'patch']
```

Custom serializer is also affect on serialization of resource schema document. In example case /api/v1/tag/schema/ looks like:

```json
{
    "defaultLimit": 20,
    "fields": {
        "name": {
            "nullable": false,
            "default": "",
            "readonly": false,
            "helpText": "Unicode string data. Ex: \"Hello World\"",
            "blank": true,
            "unique": false,
            "type": "string"
        }
    },
    "defaultFormat": "application/json",
    "allowedListHttpMethods": [
        "get",
        "post"
    ],
    "allowedDetailHttpMethods": [
        "get",
        "put",
        "delete",
        "patch"
    ]
}
```

I think that setting custom JSON serializer shouldn't affect schema document, but only serialization of resource itself.

issue_title: Dynamic delta T for solar position calculation
issue_body: The solar position algorithm from NREL needs a delta t parameter to account for the changes in Earth's rotation. issue_title: Volume mount erase_on_boot flag interprets non-empty strings as true by default
issue_body:
When deploying a stack backed by ebs volume using senza, the senza.yaml config interprets a non-empty string in `erase_on_boot` flag as true. For instance the following config 
```
mounts:
   /data:
      partition: /dev/xvdk
      filesystem: ext4
      erase_on_boot: "false"
      options: noatime,nodiratime,nobarrier
```
has resulted in our persisted data being wiped on every reboot. It would be great if there could be some check for this since it is very easy to miss and hard to troubleshoot.

To reproduce it is enough to do:
```python
app = DaVinci(version='v39r0p1')
app.extraopts = 'from Configurables import DaVinci; DaVinci(DataType="2015")'
app.prepare()
```

I think the issue is with the way ganga tries to read the pickled option file at
https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/0b796886d15f843d38e1a85bbb6078f0677dca93/python/GangaLHCb/Lib/Applications/PythonOptionsParser.py#L66

The problem is that gaudirun.py is not called in the correct environment (DaVinci), thus reading of the pickle fails since various classes are not available. See this little demo outside ganga:

```sh
lb-run DaVinci v39r0p1 gaudirun.py --option "from Configurables import DaVinci; DaVinci(DataType='2015')" --output test.pkl -n
# the following fails as in ganga
python -c "import cPickle; cPickle.load(file('test.pkl'))"
# this one works
lb-run DaVinci v39r0p1 python -c "import cPickle; cPickle.load(file('test.pkl'))"
```

issue_title: Killing a subjob updates the master status
issue_body:
Killing a single subjob updates the status of the master jobs and all the other subjobs to killed.

Only the statuses are updated - the other subjobs still run and complete.

issue_title: hmac digest breaking change in 1.13.1
issue_body: hmac was stored as a hex encoded string, but is now stored as a hex encoded binary value. This change appears unintentional, and secrets stored this way cannot be read using older versions of credstash or other implementations.

issue_title: Question: How to use Jupyter + SparkR and custom R install
issue_body:
I am using a Dockerized image and Jupyter notebook along with SparkR kernel. When I create a SparkR notebook, it uses an install of Microsoft R (3.3.2) instead of vanilla CRAN R install (3.2.3).

The Docker image I'm using installs some custom R libraries and Python pacakages but I don't explicitly install Microsoft R. Regardless of whether or not I can remove Microsoft R or have it side-by-side, how I can get my SparkR kernel to use a custom installation of R?

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/46286021/how-to-use-jupyter-sparkr-and-custom-r-install

issue_title: Erroneous log file name
issue_body: The default log file name of the WebUI is currenlty set to `alignak-alignak-webui.log`. As a wrokaround, se the filename in the *settings.cfg* file ...

```python

class IntroduceShip(relay.ClientIDMutation):

    class Input:
        ship_name = graphene.String(required=True)
#ship_age

    ship = graphene.Field(Ship)

#ship_age

        faction_id = input.get('faction_id')

        return IntroduceShip(ship=ship, faction=faction)


```

issue_title: REED and FED receiving of multicasts from neighboring routers
issue_body:
According to section 4.7.7.4 of thread spec 1.1.1 REEDs and FEDs must maintain one-way links with neighboring routers so that the REED/FED can receive MAC-secured multicasts from them. Currently in openthread FEDs/REEDs use link requests & accepts as required, but drop incoming MAC-secured transmissions that are not from their parents.

This is because the method of validating a MAC source address uses GetNeighbor(), which switches on the current role:
https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/1f6528e6c9a600b7f04128ed8f22e841107361f6/src/core/mac/mac.cpp#L1572-L1576
Of course, this behaviour of GetNeighbor is usually needed, so perhaps a special case in Mac.cpp to check the router table too, in the case of an FFD child receiving a broadcast frame?

Example Logs (I used an address query from a non-parent router to generate):
Child establishing one way link:
```
[6] [INFO]-MLE-----: Receive Advertisement (fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f,0x8000)
[6] [INFO]-MLE-----: Send Link Request (fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f)
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: Sent IPv6 UDP msg, len:92, chksum:1cb5, to:2e1859bb93779a0f, sec:no, prio:high
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: src: fe80:0:0:0:1432:68b9:eb2d:53b7
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: dst: fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: Received IPv6 UDP msg, len:97, chksum:8e81, from:2e1859bb93779a0f, sec:no, prio:low, rss:-20.0
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: src: fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: dst: fe80:0:0:0:1432:68b9:eb2d:53b7
[6] [INFO]-MLE-----: Receive Link Accept (fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f,0x8000)
```

Router table of child:
```
router table[6] [INFO]---------: execute command: router table

| ID | RLOC16 | Next Hop | Path Cost | LQ In | LQ Out | Age | Extended MAC     |
+----+--------+----------+-----------+-------+--------+-----+------------------+
| 29 | 0x7400 |       29 |         1 |     0 |      0 |  41 | 0000000000000000 |
| 32 | 0x8000 |        0 |         0 |     3 |      3 |   4 | 2e1859bb93779a0f |

Done
```

And the filtering upon receiving of a multicast:
```
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: Frame rx failed, error:UnknownNeighbor, len:65, seqnum:195, type:Data, src:0x8000, dst:0xffff, sec:yes, ackreq:no
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: Frame rx failed, error:UnknownNeighbor, len:65, seqnum:196, type:Data, src:0x8000, dst:0xffff, sec:yes, ackreq:no
```

issue_title: Split for rollback command output causes exception when generating diffs
issue_body:
https://github.com/napalm-automation/napalm/blob/0a2a5425e59e939efdc162a841281f01f4bc308a/napalm/nxos/nxos.py#L261

So the `#` in this split statement causes exception in NXOS 7.0(3)I7(2) on NX9300s I was testing today since there's no # in the CLI output.    I came across it when I noticed that "diffs: true" on a napalm_ansible playbook crashed, but disabling diffs pass for the platform.  

I don't have older releases on-hand to compare and determine if simply altering the split statement is adequate (that's what I've done for my local copy for now), or if we need to instead support both options with and without the `#` for various versions/platforms.  I'm happy to submit a PR for the fix, just looking for guidance on how you'd prefer it be tackled.   I'd assume a simple try-block around the split to try the old way and fall-back to the new way would suffice, like so:
```
try:
    diff_out = diff_out.split(
        '#Generating Rollback Patch')[1].replace(
            'Rollback Patch is Empty', '').strip()
except IndexError:
    # newer NXOS releases do not include the # sign in the rollback patch
     diff_out = diff_out.split(
         'Generating Rollback Patch')[1].replace(
             'Rollback Patch is Empty', '').strip()
```

issue_title: Better error checking for load command
issue_body:
It would be nice if the ``load`` command had a little bit better error checking and reporting.  Ideally it would check to make sure it is a text file, either ASCII or UTF-8 encoded. - File doesn't exist or is not a file

issue_title: Apache unit tests should not touch the global Apache config
issue_labels: [{'id': 258836903, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20apache', 'name': 'area: apache', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 245387015, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20documentation', 'name': 'area: documentation', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
When working on the developer docs, I came across the following text:

> Also note that the full test suite will attempt to modify your system's Apache config if your user has sudo permissions, so it should not be run on a production Apache server.

We do what?

Looking through commit history, I wonder if this info is accurate. #4211 changed the docs from talking about `apacheconftest` to the test suite run with `tox`. `apacheconftest` does modify the global config (which in my opinion is one more reason get rid of it, see #5938), but it does not run when you run `tox`.

If this information is inaccurate, we should delete it. If it is accurate, we should fix this and then delete this warning.

cc @joohoi

issue_title: Generated nginx configuration is missing IPv6 listen directive
E9997 (forbidden-global-variables) refers to functions as "variables" and refers to all variable occurances as "declarations"
issue_body:
Builtins, functions, and variables used in the global namespace are always referred to as "variables".

For example, running `python_ta` on `examples.global_variables_example`, you get: 

![global-variables-error1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5614375/26607605/5bf65556-4565-11e7-85e3-ca176a77bdc3.png)

Note how `function` and `print` are all called "variables".

---

In addition, sometimes the error message says "global variable is declared on line X" while the variable is only *used* on line X, not declared.

For example, running `python_ta` on `examples.pylint.W0201_attribute_defined_outside_init`, you get:

![global-variables-error2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5614375/26607971/0459c268-4567-11e7-9518-b132512cbd36.png)

issue_title: Improve search function of sos website.
issue_body: The search function (at the top right corner of [SoS homepage](https://vatlab.github.io/SoS/)) is currently implemented by [tipue](http://www.tipue.com/search/) in static mode. More specifically, 1. Script [index_site.py](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/js/index_site.py) is run manually (with release) to index headers of all pages to create [tipuesearch_content.js](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/tipuesearch/tipuesearch_content.js). This process used [BeautifulSoup](https://www.crummy.com/software/BeautifulSoup/) to parse our pages. We grab only headers because we do not want to create huge `tipuesearch_content.js` that takes a lot of time and ram to load, and because it is better to target the most important items (headers) of the website. 2. When the search function is called, it opens [Search.html](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/search.html) and calls [tipuesearch](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/tipuesearch/tipuesearch.js#L12) to generate result page. We need to improve the appearance and functions of the search box. In particular, because our headers (e.g. [this one](https://vatlab.github.io/SoS/doc/documentation/SoS_Step.html#Option-filetype-122) has link anchors and can be jumped upon directly, we would like to display the results better in the search page and be able to jump directly to headers from the search page. Tasks: 1. The search box and github icons are of different height and do not align well. We will need to adjust the height of the search box to match github icons in [testtab.css](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/css/testtab.css#L201). 2. Improve [index_site.py](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/js/index_site.py) is run manually (or automatically with [`release`](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/development/release) script when the html files are updated ) to generate better "text" to store the anchors. For example, instead of saving `Option filetype` for the aforementioned URL, we save `Option filetype #Option-filetype-122`. This can be achieved by better handling in the BeautifulSoup parsing. 3. Improve [tipuesearch](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/blob/master/docs/tipuesearch/tipuesearch.js#L12) to better display the output. Basically, this function will find the item and display its context. We will need to change that part to display URLs from the anchors. That is to say, for matched text `filetype` with line `Option filetype #Option-filetype-122`, we display `Option filetype`. 4. A search box has been added to each doc page but its destination is the default `search.html`. It would be better to send results to a search page with side panel so everything will be within the doc style. Current screenshot: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9889312/31046245-1c5b49f4-a5bb-11e7-954b-76cfde3290ef.png) Need to have the anchored link(s) directly under the title.

issue_title: "Group" or step_id of tasks.
issue_body: Right now tasks are independent entities. It would be good to store step name or ID in the task file so that we know what these tasks belong to. One obvious applications is that, after we execute a workflow multiple with different input and parameters, we can have a distribution of resources used for this particular task/command, so that we could adjust our `mem` and `walltime` parameters accordingly.

issue_title: JSON objects returned by asynchronous methods are not convenient
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20code', 'name': 'area: code', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587821, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20feature', 'name': 'type: feature', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The resource class methods invoking asynchronous HMC operations return the JSON object returned by the HMC if `wait_for_completion=False`. The only information returned in that object is the URI of the Job object.

Proposal: Change the return value to return a new `Job` object that is a resource object filled with just its URI, and that has a method to pull the job status.

issue_title: auth_time on refresh token
issue_body:
When ``refresh_token()`` from ``storage.py`` is called the value of ``auth_time`` is not updated (the value of the ``access_token`` and so on is updated).

My code:

    self.social = user.social_auth.get(provider='custom')
    if self.social.access_token_expired():
        strategy = load_strategy()
        self.social.refresh_token(strategy)

The value of the ``access_token`` is updated but not the ``auth_time``.


My solution was adding two lines:

    self.social.extra_data['auth_time'] = int(time.time())
    self.social.save()

But there must be a better solution because every time the token is refreshed this is assumed.

issue_body:
mail-parser currently fails to install depending on system locale (`LC_CTYPE`), like so:

```

issue_title: Occasional failure in plasma manager during reconstruction.
issue_body:
I'm encountering the following problem. I'm using Anaconda Python 3.5 and building Ray from source. The problem can be reproduced as follows.

1. Start Ray on an 11 node cluster.

2. Create a driver (I've been doing this on the head node), and run the following.

    ```python
    import ray
    import time

    ray.init(redis_address=...)

    @ray.remote
    def f():
        time.sleep(0.01)
        return ray.services.get_node_ip_address()

    %time l = ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(1000000)])
    ```

3. SSH to a different node and run `ray stop`.

Maybe half the time, the plasma manager on the head node will exit with the following error.

```
[FATAL] (/home/ubuntu/ray/src/plasma/plasma_manager.cc:1494: errno: Connection refused) The last heartbeat was sent 10292 milliseconds ago.
```

cc @stephanie-wang

issue_title: Actor methods should do some checking when they are invoked instead of when they are executed.
issue_body:
Currently, if we do

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.actor
class Foo(object):
  def __init__(self):
    pass
  def get_val(self, x):
    return x

f = Foo()
```

And then we call `f.get_val()`, the method will be executed as a task on the actor and will throw an exception. However, we could have thrown an exception on the caller side as we do with remote functions. For example,

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
def f(x):
  return 1

f.remote()
```

Fails with

issue_title: Intermittent failures in `test_open_tcp_listeners_backlog` on Travis MacOS
issue_body:
We seem to be getting intermittent failures in `test_open_tcp_listeners_backlog`:

https://travis-ci.org/python-trio/trio/jobs/409715108
https://travis-ci.org/python-trio/trio/jobs/409715111

This is a test where we open a listening port with a specific backlog, then try to connect to it and make sure its backlog actually is at least as large as we requested. In those two logs we request a backlog of 11, and one time we manage 7 connections and the other time we manage 3.

Ideas:

* The way we detect the backlog being full is that we attempt to connect, and after 0.5 seconds the connection still hasn't succeeded. I guess the host could be so overloaded that somehow connecting to a loopback socket actually takes 0.5 seconds sometimes? It seems unlikely that we're getting random 0.5 second glitches so often that they hit this test 2 times out of 3 though...

* Currently that test is actually opening a world-readable port. Maybe someone else is randomly connecting? Seems pretty unlikely as well though... 5+ connections in a fraction of a second to a newly opened random high port, for a vps on a private network?

I guess we could disable the test entirely... it's been a hassle before, and mostly it's just checking that the value we set as `backlog` does actually get passed down to the kernel. The logic for this isn't *totally* trivial (see `_compute_backlog`), but still, not very scary. Plus setting small backlogs is probably never useful, we only support it because it's traditional.

issue_title: wall clock timing functionality
issue_body:
This is probably fairly low priority, but: an interesting and sometimes-useful feature is the ability to sleep until a particular *wall clock* time (e.g., "this cert is expiring at 2017-12-31T12:00:00Z, so I want to wake up 3 days before that so I can renew it"). This is quite different from trio's current timekeeping abilities, which are all oriented around monotonic time (which ignores clock changes, and stops while the computer is suspended, etc.). Really these are just different incommensurable time scales.

Fortunately, I don't *think* we need the ability to directly set a cancel scope deadline to a particular wall clock time, so this doesn't need to be integrated deep into the guts of trio's run loop. I think it'd be sufficient to provide a `sleep_until_wall_clock_time` (and maybe `current_wall_clock_time` for completeness).

[This comment and the replies](https://github.com/python-trio/trio/issues/168#issuecomment-304209331) have some more details on this idea, including notes on how it could be implemented on different systems.

issue_title: Report running state via AppFutures and DataFutures
issue_body:
Reported by @djf604

Currently we only have 3 states reported by future types:
1. Not done
2. Done
3. Done with Exception.

For long running applications, potentially with several stages it would be useful to report additional states. This should be queriable in non-blocking way from the Future types. For instance, we could 
support a set of reported state transitions like : `pending`, `launched`, `running`, `completed`, `failed`

issue_title: Allow deployment of single JAR for Java Lambdas
issue_body:
AWS supports two forms of archives for deploying Java Lambda functions:
1. "Fat" JAR - single jar file with all dependencies
2. ZIP - zip archive with class files in `/` and jar files in `/lib`

http://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/lambda-java-how-to-create-deployment-package.html

LocalStack currently expects a single JAR file within a ZIP file, which is incompatible with what AWS expects.

Use the --help option to list the available object types and actions:
  $ gitlab --help
  $ gitlab project --help
```

In reality:
```shell
$ gitlab --version
1.3.0
 $ gitlab --help
usage: gitlab [-h] [--version] [-v] [-d] [-c CONFIG_FILE] [-g GITLAB]
              [-o {json,legacy,yaml}] [-f FIELDS]

GitLab API Command Line Interface

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             Display the version.
  -v, --verbose, --fancy
                        Verbose mode (legacy format only)
  -d, --debug           Debug mode (display HTTP requests)
  -c CONFIG_FILE, --config-file CONFIG_FILE
                        Configuration file to use. Can be used multiple times.
  -g GITLAB, --gitlab GITLAB
                        Which configuration section should be used. If not
                        defined, the default selection will be used.
  -o {json,legacy,yaml}, --output {json,legacy,yaml}
                        Output format (v4 only): json|legacy|yaml
  -f FIELDS, --fields FIELDS
                        Fields to display in the output (comma separated). Not
                        used with legacy output
```

In short, I would like to find out what commands I can run! :)

issue_title: Default parameter value behavior like JModelica
issue_body:
Quote from #110:

What happens now is that parameters of the form
```Modelica
parameter Real a = 1.0;
```
get their numerical value exposed.

OTOH parameters like
```Modelica
parameter Real b = a;
```
do not. 

To mimic JModelica, we can return 1.0 when users request the (default) value of 'b', instead of returning "a".

issue_title: graph.query should return collections.namedtuple
issue_body:
drewpca, 2009-03-24T00:09:05.000Z 
graph.query returns a sequence of tuples, but for almost no cost, and full
backward compatibility, they could be collections.namedtuple objects instead.

Then we can write code like this:

for row in graph.query("SELECT ?huge ?list ?of ?vars ..."):
   print row.huge, row.vars
   print repr(row) # nicer than before

I would expect unbound variables to still be in the tuple, as they are now.

issue_title: ImportError: No module named term
issue_body:
kaska...@gmail.com, 2011-09-27T23:17:41.000Z 
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. hg clone https://code.google.com/p/rdflib/ 
2. mv rdflib/rdflib /tmp/extras
3. touch /tmp/extras/__init__.py
4. execute python from /tmp directory and execute: from extras import rdflib

What is the expected output? 
no output, just rdflib loaded

What do you see instead?
Python 2.7.1+ (r271:86832, Apr 11 2011, 18:13:53) 
[GCC 4.5.2] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from extras import rdflib
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "", line 1, in 
  File "extras/rdflib/__init__.py", line 64, in 
    from rdflib.term import URIRef, BNode, Literal, Variable
ImportError: No module named term


What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
Using last source from hg repository on ubuntu 11.04

Please provide any additional information below.
Pending to test in other environments. I use to follow the same steps with previous version of rdflib (2.4) with no problem at all.
Thanks in advance. I love rdflib.

Comment 1 by gjhigg...@gmail.com
If I understand it correctly, your approach relies on Python<=2.5 behaviour in which relative imports were the default. In Python>=2.6 that default has been changed in favour of absolute imports.

I don't know of any crufty, encapsulating meta-package trick that would allow you to continue your strategy uninterrupted. I was able to confirm that serial replacement of "import rdflib.X" with "import .X" does eventually result in a successful import of rdflib but I wasn't able to take it any further than that.

If there are no other options available to you then the only suggestion I can make is that you create a separate clone of rdflib, edited to perform relative imports as described above (assuming that it satisfactorily executes your tests) and to use that clone for your testing.

Sorry I can't be of more help.


Comment 2 by kaska...@gmail.com
Thanks a lot for pointing me in the right way. I hadn't noticed this change in Python. I've been reading PEP 328 (Absolute and Relative Imports) and it seems there is nothing to do. As you say Python 2.5 was the last version allowing relative imports. 

It's not a big problem for me to use absolute imports but I was happiest building my own bundles and test them in other distros or shared webservers. 

I'm not an python expert but I wish if instead:
from __future__ import absolute_import
I could use:
from __past__ import relative_import


Much appreciated.


Comment 3 by gromgull
not really a bug.

issue_title: Subqueries fail silently
issue_body:
Taken from this mailing list discussion: http://groups.google.com/group/django-non-relational/browse_thread/thread/c006d67444bb28f7

Given the models:

    from django.db import models

    class Person(models.Model):
        name = models.CharField(max_length=200)

    class Car(models.Model):
        name = models.CharField(max_length=200)
        owner = models.ForeignKey(Person)

    class Wheel(models.Model):
        part_of = models.ForeignKey(Car)

Subqueries return no results, even when there should be

    In [1]: from test_app.models import Person, Car, Wheel

    In [2]: p = Person.objects.create(name='bob')

    In [3]: c1 = Car.objects.create(name='mini', owner=p)

    In [4]: c2 = Car.objects.create(name='landrover', owner=p)

    In [5]: w = Wheel.objects.create(part_of=c2)

    In [6]: cars = Car.objects.filter(owner=p)

    In [7]: cars
    Out[7]: [<Car: Car object>, <Car: Car object>]

    In [8]: Wheel.objects.filter(part_of__in=cars) # cars is a QuerySet
    Out[8]: []

    In [9]: Wheel.objects.filter(part_of__in=list(cars)) # force QuerySet to list
    Out[9]: [<Wheel: Wheel object>]
